title: Decode-invariance factorial audit — harness + deferred audit
hypothesis: At least one durable checkpoint changes parse/meaningful/placeholder/structural outcomes under a corrected/current decoder on byte-identical weights, invalidating a prior training/architecture attribution.
falsifier: All compatible decode paths agree within preregistered equivalence bands on every checkpoint and suite.

compatibility_manifest.note: No durable checkpoints supplied: the decode-invariance audit is deferred until frontier/diagnostic checkpoints are synced (see SLM-103).

e288_decoder_defect_regression.scenario: byte-identical weights, checkpoint_declared parse 0 (19/19 empty) vs current_exact_or_compiler parse 1.0 (valid-but-trivial)

audit_blocker: Zero durable checkpoints resolvable in this environment (SLM-103 backfill: 227/231 model-card rows are unresolved-local). The ten-checkpoint factorial run requires a GPU host with synced E224+ checkpoints.
verified: decode-path registry (3 paths) + compatibility predicates + deterministic fingerprints
checkpoint x decode-path compatibility manifest + fail-closed validation
paired disagreement/attribution classifier + preregistered equivalence bands
decision: Harness delivered and validated (wiring evidence, not a ship or decoder-sensitivity claim). Actual ten-checkpoint audit + findings deferred to a credentialed GPU host with durable checkpoints; matrix-row execution generates per-checkpoint rows from the compatibility manifest at run time.
